_meta.source: hand-authored seed data, not from Firecrawl
_meta.note: Dates are approximate based on training-data recollection — verify before relying on them.

items.title: GPT-5 release
items.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5 across ChatGPT and the API, unifying the GPT and o-series reasoning models into a single product line with automatic routing.
items.why_it_matters: Ends the bifurcated GPT/o-series lineup that confused API consumers about when to pay for reasoning. Unified routing simplifies integration but gives operators less explicit control over the cost-vs-quality tradeoff per call.

items.title: Claude 4 family (Sonnet 4, Opus 4)
items.summary: Released Claude Sonnet 4 and Opus 4, the next generation of frontier models with improved reasoning, tool use, and longer effective context.
items.why_it_matters: Raised the baseline for coding and agentic workloads. If you benchmarked your product on Claude 3.5 or 3.7 to make a build-vs-buy or pricing decision, those numbers are stale and may have flipped the call.

items.title: Claude Code reaches general availability
items.summary: Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-native coding agent, reached GA with support for hooks, slash commands, and IDE integrations.
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic is now competing at the workflow layer with Cursor, Aider, and others — not just selling them the model underneath. Worth watching how this reshapes the IDE/agent vendor stack, especially for anyone building coding tools.

items.title: Llama 4 family released
items.summary: Meta released the Llama 4 family (Scout, Maverick, Behemoth) — its first natively multimodal open-weight models with mixture-of-experts architectures.
items.why_it_matters: First major MoE open-weight release from Meta — inference economics now resemble a smaller dense model while quality tracks a larger one. If you self-host, recheck your hosting math against Llama 4 before renewing GPU contracts.

items.title: Gemma 3 open-weight release
items.summary: DeepMind released Gemma 3, an open-weight family from 1B to 27B parameters with multimodal and 128K-context variants.
items.why_it_matters: Small open models keep getting good enough for production use cases that previously demanded a frontier API. Worth re-evaluating whether your AI features actually need a hosted call or could move to on-device / private inference.

items.title: Reka Flash 3 released
items.summary: Reka released Flash 3, a 21B-parameter multimodal model with reasoning performance on par with OpenAI's o1-mini at a fraction of the inference cost.
items.why_it_matters: Reasoning capability is generalizing beyond OpenAI/DeepSeek to smaller labs. Reka becomes a credible third option for cheap reasoning, especially when you also need multimodal inputs in the same call.

items.title: Constitutional Classifiers defend against jailbreaks
items.summary: Anthropic published research on constitutional classifiers that defend against universal jailbreaks while preserving model helpfulness on benign queries.
items.why_it_matters: Matters if your product depends on Claude rejecting bad inputs without becoming annoying on good ones — the classic over-refusal tax has been a real problem for consumer-facing apps built on safety-tuned models.

items.title: Deep Research agent launch
items.summary: OpenAI shipped Deep Research, a multi-step web research agent that produces analyst-style reports from a single prompt.
items.why_it_matters: Shifts the 'read sources + write a report' workflow from a human-driven multi-prompt loop to a single-prompt agent. If your product sells analyst memos, market scans, or competitive research, OpenAI now ships a built-in competitor inside ChatGPT.

items.title: Operator browsing agent launch
items.summary: OpenAI launched Operator, an agent that can browse and operate websites on a user's behalf, initially available to Pro subscribers.
items.why_it_matters: First serious consumer-facing browsing agent from a frontier lab. The 'give it a goal, watch it operate' pattern previews how a chunk of SaaS UI may get displaced — relevant for anyone whose product is essentially 'an interface that clicks things'.

items.title: DeepSeek-R1 open-weight reasoning model
items.summary: DeepSeek released R1, an open-weight reasoning model competitive with o1 at a fraction of the price, along with a detailed technical report on its RL training recipe.
items.why_it_matters: Collapsed the price floor for serious reasoning capabilities almost overnight. If you charge for reasoning-heavy AI features, your unit economics likely need a refresh. Also legitimized the open-weight reasoning category, which other labs are now racing to match.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3 (671B MoE)
items.summary: DeepSeek released V3, a 671B-parameter MoE model approaching GPT-4-class performance, reportedly trained for under $6M in compute.
items.why_it_matters: Showed that frontier-class training is possible on much smaller compute budgets than previously assumed. Forces a rethink of moats based on capex alone — and a recheck of fundraising narratives that lean on 'training cost is the moat'.

items.title: Veo 2 video generation model
items.summary: DeepMind unveiled Veo 2, a state-of-the-art video generation model with improved realism and longer-clip capabilities.
items.why_it_matters: Video generation crossed into 'useful for marketing/explainer content' territory. If your product touches video creation in any way, the bar for prompt-to-video quality just moved — and end-user expectations follow.

items.title: Gemini 2.0 announced
items.summary: DeepMind announced Gemini 2.0 with native multimodality, agent capabilities (Project Mariner, Astra), and rollout across Google products.
items.why_it_matters: Established Gemini as a peer of Claude/GPT, not a runner-up. For enterprises in the Google ecosystem, Gemini 2 is now the credible default — which affects deal economics if you're selling AI features into Google-shop accounts.

items.title: Sora launches to ChatGPT users
items.summary: OpenAI launched Sora to ChatGPT Plus and Pro users, bringing high-quality text-to-video generation to a paid consumer product.
items.why_it_matters: Brought serious text-to-video to a paid consumer surface. If you sell video tooling, expect 'why can't I just type a prompt' from users in the next sales cycle.

items.title: Llama 3.3 70B released
items.summary: Meta released Llama 3.3 70B, matching the performance of the much larger 405B model at a fraction of the inference cost.
items.why_it_matters: Strong signal that post-training and distillation matter as much as raw scale. Changes the self-hosting calculus toward smaller, cheaper deployments — and updates the baseline you should be benchmarking against.

items.title: OLMo 2 fully open model release
items.summary: AI2 released OLMo 2 (7B and 13B), fully open models including training data, code, and recipes — closing the gap with closed-weight peers.
items.why_it_matters: Fully open (data + code + weights) means OLMo is the reference if you need to deeply understand or modify model training. For research-shop startups and academic deployments, this is now the reproducible base.

items.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) open-sourced
items.summary: Anthropic open-sourced the Model Context Protocol, a standard for connecting LLM applications to external tools and data sources.
items.why_it_matters: MCP has cross-vendor adoption and is becoming the lingua franca for LLM tool/data integration. If you're building agentic apps, implementing MCP is increasingly cheaper than maintaining your own protocol — and gets you ecosystem compatibility for free.

items.title: Tülu 3 open post-training recipe
items.summary: AI2 released Tülu 3, a fully open post-training recipe applied to Llama 3.1 that surpasses Llama 3.1 Instruct on key benchmarks.
items.why_it_matters: If you fine-tune, Tülu 3 is now the baseline to compare your post-training against — and a reproducible reference for what a strong open recipe looks like end-to-end.

items.title: Aya Expanse multilingual models
items.summary: Cohere For AI released Aya Expanse, an open-weight multilingual model family covering 23 languages with strong performance on non-English benchmarks.
items.why_it_matters: Serious multilingual coverage matters for any product targeting non-English markets. Aya is currently the most credible open-weight option for strong non-English performance without going commercial.

items.title: Claude computer use (beta)
items.summary: Claude can now take screenshots and control a virtual computer, marking a first step toward general agentic capabilities in a frontier API.
items.why_it_matters: First production API for general GUI control by an LLM. Clunky and expensive today, but the trajectory matters — many product workflows are essentially 'click buttons on someone's behalf', and that surface is now addressable.

items.title: Command R+ refresh
items.summary: Cohere released an updated Command R+ with improved tool-use, RAG, and multilingual performance for enterprise applications.
items.why_it_matters: Cohere keeps doubling down on RAG-optimized models for enterprise use. If you sell to regulated industries (finance, healthcare, gov), Cohere is increasingly the realistic alternative to OpenAI/Anthropic in procurement.

items.title: 'The AI Scientist' framework published
items.summary: Sakana published 'The AI Scientist,' a framework demonstrating end-to-end automated ML research from idea to written paper, including automated peer review.
items.why_it_matters: End-to-end automated research is still rough but the demo previews where knowledge-work tooling is going. If you sell analyst tools, research assistants, or report generators, this is a 1-2 year out signal worth tracking even if it doesn't affect your product today.

items.title: AlphaProof reaches IMO silver-medal performance
items.summary: DeepMind's AlphaProof and AlphaGeometry 2 reached silver-medal performance on International Mathematical Olympiad problems, a first for AI systems.
items.why_it_matters: First credible AI performance on competitive-math-level problems. Niche today, but a signal that formal reasoning is becoming tractable — relevant for any product depending on rigorous, verifiable logic chains (legal reasoning, formal verification, financial modeling).

items.title: Mistral Large 2 released
items.summary: Mistral released Mistral Large 2 with 123B parameters and strong code, math, and multilingual performance under a research license.
items.why_it_matters: European-headquartered frontier-tier option matters for any operator selling into EU enterprises with data-residency or sovereignty requirements. Mistral remains the cleanest hedge against pure US/China dependency in procurement.

items.title: Codestral code model released
items.summary: Mistral launched Codestral, a 22B-parameter open-weight code model trained on over 80 programming languages.
items.why_it_matters: Useful baseline if you're building developer tooling on a non-US base model, or if you need to self-host code completion for IP or compliance reasons.
